title: Why balanced hybrid funds are back in focus
summary: A balanced hybrid strategy aims to keep the equity-debt allocation closer to a 50:50 mix, providing more stability and limiting downside risk.

title: How to spot fake websites: PIB Fact Check lists 5 warning signs to watch for
summary: PIB Fact Check suggests that users should verify the URL, ensure the site uses HTTPS, look for the padlock icon, avoid websites with excessive pop-ups or clickbait, check the contact and about pages, and avoid offers that appear too good to be true.

title: Tax credit mismatch in ITR? Here's how to check and fix it before your refund is delayed
summary: A tax credit mismatch occurs when the tax credit claimed in your ITR does not match the records available with the Income Tax Department. It can delay refunds, lead to tax notices, or result in additional tax demands.

title: ESIC extends Atal Beemit Vyakti Kalyan Yojana till June 2027: Who can claim unemployment benefits?
summary: Atal Beemit Vyakti Kalyan Yojana (ABVKY) under ESIC has been extended until 30 June 2027, allowing eligible ESI-insured employees to continue receiving temporary unemployment allowance during job loss and financial hardship.

title: Investing apps demand scrutiny: Why choosing the right platform is about more than pricing
summary: Smart investing apps promise convenience, but they demand scrutiny. From transparency in fees to clarity in advice, investors must research carefully, verify SEBI registrations and treat apps as tools, not decision‑makers, to avoid costly mistakes.&nbsp;

title: ₹30 lakh salary doesn't mean a 30% tax rate: Here's why effective tax rate is just 15.86% under new tax regime
summary: A ₹30 lakh annual salary doesn't mean your entire income is taxed at 30%. After the ₹75,000 standard deduction under the new regime, slab-wise taxation, and a 4% health and education cess, the total tax liability comes to ₹475,800, resulting in an effective tax rate of just 15.86%.

title: EPF interest after retirement at 58 continues until 61. Here's right time to withdraw your savings
summary: An EPF account can continue earning interest for up to 3 years after retirement at age 58. Knowing the right time to withdraw your EPF savings helps maximise returns before the account becomes inoperative.

title: 25% chance of Sensex hitting 1 lakh in next 1 year: Morgan Stanley's Ridham Desai
summary: Morgan Stanley's Ridham Desai sees a 25% chance of the Sensex touching 1 lakh within the next year, driven by stronger earnings and improving macroeconomic fundamentals. The brokerage's base case targets 89,000 by June 2027, while assigning an equal 25% probability to a bear-case decline to 66,000.
